items.title: 100,000 exotic cockroaches seized from alleged Bathurst breeder
items.summary: More than 100,000 live exotic cockroaches have allegedly been seized from a commercial breeder in Central West NSW.

items.title: Australian bull semen exports to China resume after pandemic pause
items.summary: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

items.title: ASIC has 8 contracts with KPMG despite audit scandal, ASX loses ground — as it happened
items.summary: ASIC tells Senate estimates it has eight active contracts with KPMG, despite the company facing an audit scandal. Meanwhile, the ASX lost ground. Catch up on the day's events and insights in our live markets blog.

items.title: Mirra Andreeva to face Polish qualifier Maja Chwalińska in women's French Open final
items.summary: Nineteen-year-old Mirra Andreeva reaches her first grand slam final at the French Open where she will take on Poland's Maja Chwalińska, the first qualifier to reach the Paris decider in the Open Era.

items.title: Nauiyu leaders race to bring services back online as first residents return to flood-ravaged community
items.summary: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

items.title: PM says he won't engage in 'drawn out' changes to tax reform plan — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says the government's tax reforms are proof it is "not here to simply occupy the space or pass the time".

items.title: WA's new Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz confident state's Ebola risk is low
items.summary: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

items.title: Torrington community to discuss security concerns after alleged home invasion
items.summary: The Torrington community will meet to discuss security measures following an alleged home invasion and a couple's stabbing.

items.title: Australia loses third ODI to drop series in Pakistan after another poor batting display in Lahore
items.summary: Pakistan comfortably chases down 158 to win the third one-day international in Lahore, beating Australia 2-1 in their series.

items.title: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour sat down for an exclusive interview with Stateline NT in Alice Springs.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Light shed on how Kumanjayi Little Baby ended up in a town camp in Alice Springs the night she disappeared
items.summary: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill four
items.summary: Iran has made a ceasefire in Lebanon a condition for any peace deal with Washington, and has suggested in recent days that it could intervene directly if Israel keeps up attacks there.

items.title: Falling house prices would ease inflation but politicians find the question much more complicated
items.summary: Politicians have been hesitant to say whether prices should rise or fall, as both sides of politics feel the pressure of trying to appease voters who own and voters who are trying to buy.

items.title: Broken Hill pays homage to champion of outback's Silver City Cinema
items.summary: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

items.title: No surprises but risks aplenty in Tom Koutsantonis's comeback budget
items.summary: Some things haven't changed since Labor Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis last handed down a state budget in 2017.

items.title: Federal government hopes bulk-billing contraceptive centres will empower more women
items.summary: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

items.title: Australia's Kaitlin Shave has 'unfinished business' after injury ended 2024 Paris Olympics debut
items.summary: For an athlete still in the early stages of her professional career, Australian rugby 7s player Kaitlin Shave has already experienced the highs and lows of sport.

items.title: Fighting the spread of Ebola virus in one of the world's 'most volatile' regions
items.summary: Tensions are running high as officials fight to stop the rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where aid workers tell the ABC they are already playing catch-up with the deadly disease.

items.title: Public apology after Nepal's women's national team violates 'spirit of cricket'
items.summary: Nepal's cricket board has offered a public apology after its women's team successfully had a batter timed out.

items.title: Frustration over approach to dealing with Indian myna driving grassroots action
items.summary: In one Queensland town, a wildlife carer is so concerned about the Indian myna's threat to native species, she has self-funded a bounty program that offers $5 for every bird humanely caught.

items.title: One Nation cost former Queensland premier Rob Borbidge office. This is his warning for the current government
items.summary: Former Nationals premier of Queensland Rob Borbidge says alarm bells are ringing within conservative ranks over surging support for One Nation, but he believes there is still time to prevent what some opinion polls suggest could be an electoral wipe-out.

items.title: Farmer says flood plain harvesting has forced him to run cattle by the roadside
items.summary: A western New South Wales farmer says dams built next door have stopped all water flows onto his property, forcing him to graze and water his stock by the side of the road.

items.title: Quit Victoria aims new campaign at vapers while warning laws fall short
items.summary: Anti-vaping advocates have launched a new campaign warning young people of the health dangers posed by vaping. They say laws in Victoria are not helping their cause.

items.title: RSV Nuyina can't access Hobart's marine refuelling depot, so what are its other options?
items.summary: Australia's Antarctic icebreaker can't access the marine fuel depot near its home port in Hobart. It currently makes a 674 kilometre detour to Burnie, but are there other options?

items.title: Australia's greenhouse gas emissions drop as renewable energy, batteries surge
items.summary: Emissions are at their lowest point since the COVID pandemic shut down the economy, dropping 2.1 per cent over the year to December 2025.

items.title: Brumbies, Reds must create Super Rugby Pacific history in New Zealand to keep finals hopes alive
items.summary: History is stacked against the Brumbies and Reds surviving week one of the Super Rugby Pacific finals, which kick off on Friday evening.

items.title: KPMG audits the RBA and runs its whistleblower hotline but maybe not for much longer
items.summary: The Reserve Bank is distancing itself from KPMG with Governor Michele Bullock confirming the bank will re-tender its whistleblower hotline service.

items.title: Hanson prepares to take the big stage as Chalmers's budget backlash rolls on
items.summary: While Labor is increasingly concerned about the long-term threat of One Nation, this week its attention was squarely on more immediate problems.

items.title: Oscar Piastri seeking Monaco success as McLaren marks 1,000 F1 grands prix
items.summary: On an historic occasion for one of Formula 1's most storied teams, Oscar Piastri is hoping to add silverware to McLaren's special day in Monaco.

items.title: China warns Australia that 55 per cent beef tariff could apply within days
items.summary: China is cracking down on beef imports and says it is ready to slap Australia with an additional 55 per cent tariff in the coming days.

items.title: Abnormal moles seen developing in patients chasing perfect suntan by using peptide melanotan-II
items.summary: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

items.title: India's Great Nicobar Island Project goes ahead despite concerns about isolated Shompen tribe
items.summary: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: Rumours, rifts and a reported resignation form a murky picture of a war-ravaged Iran
items.summary: A report about the resignation of Iran's president earlier this week came at a turbulent moment for Iran's regime, which is grappling with frictions between moderates and hardliners as the war drags on.

items.title: Fuel price expected to rise after government's excise cuts end on June 30
items.summary: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

items.title: The best seasonal produce to look for in June
items.summary: Winter has arrived. This is some of the best seasonal produce to look for.

items.title: Debuting at the Sydney Film Festival, Grace McKenzie's Yumburra is a lyrical ode to doing things differently
items.summary: A slow-cinema treasure, Grace McKenzie's documentary, Yumburra, traces author Bruce Pascoe's establishment of a farm.

items.title: ABC news quiz: Mystery markings, a premature celebration and a fight over the Big … something
items.summary: You too could learn the dangers of celebrating too soon if you underestimate this week's news quiz.

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining classified information, sources say
items.summary: The former national security adviser, who served in Donald Trump's first administration, is now a prominent critic of the president.

items.title: Penny Wong says she believes activists who say they were sexually assaulted in Israeli custody
items.summary: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has told a parliamentary hearing she believes the allegations of Australian activists, who say they were sexually assaulted while detained by Israeli forces.

items.title: Government takes PFAS environmental contamination case to court
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Advocates raise concerns over child safety inquiry's recommendations to elevate adoption in permanency hierarchy
items.summary: Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into Child Safety report, released by the state government on Wednesday, found the availability of adoption should "not be limited by cultural background or ethnicity".

items.title: Bondi Beach terror attack hero Ahmed Al Ahmed charged with assault
items.summary: Ahmed Al Ahmed has been charged with domestic violence common assault after an incident in Bankstown in March but told the ABC "it's not true at all".

items.title: WA 'spud king' Tony Galati's business fined $20k for unlicensed chip factory
items.summary: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
items.summary: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Ex-WA teacher Kurt Charlton jailed for sexually abusing children he was babysitting
items.summary: Kurt David Charlton, a former WA teacher, is sentenced for sexual offences against nine victims, who were all aged under 13 at the time.

items.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Souths stung by 'sticky spray' ruling as Manly continue Foran resurgence
items.summary: The Manly Sea Eagles have provisionally moved into the NRL top four with a 28-14 win over the South Sydney Rabbitohs at Brookvale Oval.

items.title: Australian woman faces jail time over alleged cannabis vape liquid in Indonesia
items.summary: The woman reportedly told officers she used the cannabis liquid to treat knee pain and depression, but authorities say she faces up to 20 years in jail and a hefty fine.

items.title: RBA not concerned about stagflation or wage-price spiral
items.summary: RBA governor Michele Bullock says she is not concerned about stagflation or a wage-price spiral

items.title: Former Married at First Sight Australia bride Tahnee Cook says she felt pressure to be intimate on the show
items.summary: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

items.title: 'Did he just swear?': Senate estimates rolls on, government under pressure
items.summary: This week the Albanese government was under pressure on multiple fronts, Senate Estimates got a little sweary and Tony Abbott said he hadn't taken a "vow of silence".

items.title: Full Circle launches Adelaide musical theatre company with Catch Me If You Can
items.summary: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

items.title: As Beijing cracks down on VPNs, internet users in China find ways around the Great Firewall
items.summary: People in China say it is increasingly difficult to access the open internet, but they continue to find ways around Beijing's censorship apparatus.

items.title: EPA pushes back on need for real-time data on antibiotic use in Tasmania's salmon pens
items.summary: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

items.title: Sacked Sydney nurses argue video showing alleged antisemitic threats was recorded illegally
items.summary: A lawyer acting for one of two Sydney nurses accused of making antisemitic threats via an online video chat app has told a court their actions were "stupid things said by stupid people".

items.title: Argentinian teen's brutal death shakes nation as thousands demand justice for women
items.summary: The alleged brutal murder of 14-year-old Agostina Vega in Argentina has reignited a generation-defining movement demanding justice for the women victims of crime and violence.

items.title: Queensland minister Tim Nicholls 'acknowledges trauma' of Wolston Park survivors but does not apologise
items.summary: Victims of alleged abuse at a Queensland mental health facility have been left disappointed after the state's health minister did not offer a public apology.

items.title: Greens question whether Jim Chalmers could exempt home owners in his electorate from CGT changes
items.summary: While the party has indicated it is willing to help Labor pass the tax laws — which includes changes to negative gearing, capital gains tax (CGT) discount, trusts and the $250 tax offset — by early July, the Greens hold concerns about specifics in the bill.

items.title: Victorian vehicle theft rates rise to buck trend across mainland Australia
items.summary: Victoria has higher vehicle insurance claims over stolen cars than all other mainland states combined, new data from the Insurance Council of Australia reveals.

items.title: Lyell McEwin Hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours amid pay dispute
items.summary: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

items.title: Socceroos emerging star Lucas Herrington cruising into maiden World Cup
items.summary: Lucas Herrington is on track to become Australia's youngest World Cup starter yet, but the 18-year-old is taking his incredible rise in his stride.

items.title: Aged Care Minister Sam Rae grilled over human involvement in aged care assessments
items.summary: Aged Care Minister Sam Rae has strongly defended an assessment tool for at-home funding support after ordering its urgent review.

items.title: Four New Zealand MPs banned from China, Hong Kong and Macau after Taiwan trip
items.summary: Beijing's move to ban four New Zealand MPs from travelling to China, Hong Kong and Macau is largely unprecedented, causing alarm in Canberra and Wellington.

items.title: Pat Cummins hopeful of playing every Test of Australia's hectic 2026/27 schedule
items.summary: Australian men's captain Pat Cummins says his body is feeling "awesome" ahead of a hectic 12-month stretch of Test cricket.

items.title: Beekeepers race to save hundreds of beehives after 'confronting' crash
items.summary: A crew of nine beekeepers from across southern Queensland is rushing to save thousands of bees after the truck carrying their hives crashed on the Warrego Highway.
